title: Create new dashboard app shell alongside legacy dashboard.html
description: Introduce a new dashboard app structure (routes + layout + components folder) next to website/dashboard.html without modifying the legacy HTML.

steps.instruction: Inspect website/dashboard.html to understand the existing layout (nav, header, main content). Create a new dashboard app shell under an appropriate framework-specific directory (for example, app/dashboard/page.tsx and components/dashboard/Layout.tsx if the project uses Next.js, or src/pages/dashboard/index.tsx and src/components/dashboard/Layout.tsx if it uses a CRA/Vite-style React app). The new shell should roughly mirror the structure of the legacy page (sidebar/header/content), but should not yet include real data.

If the repo already has an established routing/layout pattern, adapt the new dashboard route and layout to match that convention instead of inventing a new one. Ensure there is a single entry point for the new dashboard (e.g. /dashboard) that renders the Layout and a placeholder for Overview content.

Add a brief comment or small markdown note in the repo (e.g. docs/dashboard-migration-notes.md) summarizing where the new dashboard app shell lives and how it relates to the legacy website/dashboard.html, referencing the migration blueprint.

Run the project type-check or build command (if present) to ensure the new dashboard shell compiles. Use an appropriate command such as npm run build, npm run lint, or npm run type-check depending on what exists in package.json.
commands: npm run lint || npm run build || npm test || echo 'no-check'
